initial_program:
% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Grid dimensions
% --------------------------------------------------------------
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _) }.
row(R)  :- R = 0..N-1.
col(C)  :- C = 0..M-1.

% Output cell predicate (exactly one per cell)
output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Flanked cells
%   A cell (R,C) is flanked by colour X if there is a nonzero X
%   somewhere to the left of C (cyclically) and a nonzero X somewhere
%   to the right of C (cyclically).  The two appearances may be on
%   opposite sides of the row (wraparound).
% --------------------------------------------------------------
flanked(R,C,X) :-
    row(R), col(C),
    ( col(L), col(L) < C, input(R,L,X), col(Rc), col(Rc) > C,
      input(R,Rc,X) ) ;
    ( col(L), col(L) > C, input(R,L,X), col(Rc), col(Rc) < C,
      input(R,Rc,X) ).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Transformation table (what a flanked colour becomes)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
transform(8,4).
transform(3,6).
transform(6,3).
transform(4,2).
transform(2,8).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Exactly one output colour per cell
% --------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,Color) : output_cell(R,C), color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Constraints that enforce the hidden rule
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% Flanked cells must take the transformed colour and cannot keep the
% original colour.
:- output(R,C,Y), flanked(R,C,X), Y != transform(X).
:- output(R,C,Y), flanked(R,C,X), Y != X.

% Nonflanked zeros stay zero.
:- output(R,C,Y), input(R,C,0), not flanked(R,C), Y != 0.

% Nonflanked nonzero cells keep their colour.
:- output(R,C,Y), input(R,C,X), X != 0, not flanked(R,C), Y != X.

% A cell cannot be flanked by two different colours  it can have at most
% one colour X that appears on both sides.
:- flanked(R,C,X), flanked(R,C,Y), X != Y.

#show output/3.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Grid dimensions
% --------------------------------------------------------------
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _) }.
row(R)  :- R = 0..N-1.
col(C)  :- C = 0..M-1.

% Output cell predicate (exactly one per cell)
output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Flanked cells
%   A cell (R,C) is flanked by colour X if there is a nonzero X
%   somewhere to the left of C (cyclically) and a nonzero X somewhere
%   to the right of C (cyclically).  The two appearances may be on
%   opposite sides of the row (wraparound).
% --------------------------------------------------------------
flanked(R,C,X) :-
    row(R), col(C),
    ( col(L), col(L) < C, input(R,L,X), col(Rc), col(Rc) > C,
      input(R,Rc,X) ) ;
    ( col(L), col(L) > C, input(R,L,X), col(Rc), col(Rc) < C,
      input(R,Rc,X) ).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Transformation table (what a flanked colour becomes)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
transform(8,4).
transform(3,6).
transform(6,3).
transform(4,2).
transform(2,8).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Exactly one output colour per cell
% --------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,Color) : output_cell(R,C), color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Constraints that enforce the hidden rule
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% Flanked cells must take the transformed colour and cannot keep the
% original colour.
:- output(R,C,Y), flanked(R,C,X), Y != transform(X).
:- output(R,C,Y), flanked(R,C,X), Y != X.

% Nonflanked zeros stay zero.
:- output(R,C,Y), input(R,C,0), not flanked(R,C), Y != 0.

% Nonflanked nonzero cells keep their colour.
:- output(R,C,Y), input(R,C,X), X != 0, not flanked(R,C), Y != X.

% A cell cannot be flanked by two different colours  it can have at most
% one colour X that appears on both sides.
:- flanked(R,C,X), flanked(R,C,Y), X != Y.

#show output/3.
